common.unsaved_title: You have unsaved changes — click Save to commit them.

session_secret_auto.title: SESSION_SECRET not set — sessions will not survive a restart
session_secret_auto.body: OmniGrid generated an ephemeral session-signing key at boot because the SESSION_SECRET env var is unset. Every container restart invalidates every active session (yours included), forcing all users to log in again. Set SESSION_SECRET in /app/.env to a stable random value to fix this.
session_secret_auto.dismiss_title: Dismiss for this browser session (will reappear after the next OmniGrid restart)

bootstrap_env_still_set.title: Bootstrap admin credentials still in /app/.env
bootstrap_env_still_set.body: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USER and BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD are still set in your .env file. The seed path is now a no-op (users already exist), but a wiped DB would silently re-seed an admin from these values, AND the password is sitting plaintext on disk. Blank both vars in /app/.env and redeploy.
bootstrap_env_still_set.dismiss_title: Dismiss for this browser session (will reappear after the next OmniGrid restart until the env vars are cleared)

errors.OG0001: DNS resolution failed — the hostname could not be resolved. Check the URL, container DNS (/etc/resolv.conf), split-horizon DNS, or an outbound VPN.
errors.OG0002: Connection refused by the upstream host — check the port and that the upstream service is running.
errors.OG0004: TLS handshake with the upstream failed — check the certificate or disable VERIFY_TLS for self-signed endpoints.
errors.OG0005: Host unreachable — no route from OmniGrid to the target. The host may be powered off, disconnected, or behind a firewall that blocks the source IP.
errors.OG0100: Upstream rejected the credentials (401). Token may be expired, revoked, or minted under a different tenant.
errors.OG0102: Credentials are incomplete — configure every required field.
errors.OG0202: Upstream response was not valid JSON.
errors.OG0300: Failed to write the asset inventory cache file.
errors.OG0301: Invalid range — min_value must be less than or equal to max_value.

topbar.refresh_picker_title: How the dashboard stays current. Exactly ONE of: "Live" — connect to the event stream, every chart updates instantly via push (recommended); "Off" — close the event stream and stop polling, the dashboard becomes a static snapshot; or a cadence (30s / 1m / 5m) — close the event stream and poll every chart at that rate. Switching modes immediately opens or closes the connection.
topbar.refresh_live_title: Open the event stream and update every chart on push. Closes when you pick Off or an interval.
topbar.refresh_title: Manual refresh (r) — re-runs the active view's data fetch. Use when you want fresh data NOW without waiting for the next interval tick.

events.connected_title: Real-time event stream connected — live updates via /api/events. Polling loops are idle while this stream is healthy.
events.disconnected_title: Real-time event stream not connected — using polling as a fallback. The SPA will automatically switch back to live updates when the stream recovers.
events.reconnecting_title: Real-time event stream dropped — polling has resumed and the browser is auto-reconnecting in the background.
events.off_title: Refresh is set to Off — the dashboard is a static snapshot. Pick Live or an interval from the topbar to resume updates.
events.polling_interval_title: Polling every {seconds} seconds. The pill flashes green while a fetch is in flight; grey between ticks.
events.dropped_title: {count} live events have been dropped this session because the bus's per-subscriber queue (256 events) was full. The SPA reconciled via REST after each drop, so the data is correct — this counter is a heads-up that bursts have exceeded queue capacity. Reset on page reload.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Choose which event notifications you receive. Toggles greyed out have been disabled globally by an admin.

filters.expand_collapse_title: Expand / collapse every stack (shortcuts: e / c)

nodes.docker_disk_tooltip: Docker daemon footprint: image layers + container writable layers + volumes + build cache
nodes.docker_disk_caption: Docker-only. Enable Host stats (node-exporter) in Settings for real host disk usage.
nodes.host_disk_tooltip: Real host disk usage across all non-pseudo mounts (node-exporter).
nodes.stats_all_failed_title: All configured host-stats sources failed — falling back to Docker-only signals.

empty.no_nodes: No nodes visible — check the Portainer connection.

portainer_not_configured.title: Portainer connection not configured
portainer_not_configured.body: OmniGrid needs Portainer URL + API key to discover services and containers. Set them in Admin → Portainer.

empty.no_history: No history entries match the current filter.
empty.no_backups: No backups yet — click Backup Now above.

history.title: Audit Log
history.clear_confirm_html: This will permanently delete all past update records.

settings.title: Settings
settings.readonly_banner: You're signed in as readonly — settings are viewable but can't be changed. Ask an admin if you need edits.

host_stats.title: Providers
host_stats.desc: Configure the data sources OmniGrid uses to surface host telemetry, reachability, and update state. Pick any combination of: node-exporter (Prometheus :9100 scrape), Beszel (agent + Hub), Pulse (Proxmox VE / PBS / PMG), Webmin (Miniserv module probes), and Ping (TCP/ICMP reachability + RTT). Each provider contributes what it knows; the merge layer surfaces a unified view per host.
host_stats.chip_color_hint: Colour applied to this provider's chip in the Hosts row + drawer's Enabled agents card. Leave at the default unless two providers' chips are visually too close.
host_stats.source_invalid: Data source must be one of: none, node_exporter, beszel, pulse.
host_stats.url_hint: Placeholders: {host} = Docker hostname (append a domain suffix for FQDN: http://{host}.example.com:9100/metrics), {ip} = Swarm-advertised IP (skip DNS entirely: http://{ip}:9100/metrics). Change the port here if your exporter listens somewhere other than :9100.
host_stats.placeholder_required: URL template must contain {host} or {ip} placeholder.
host_stats.overrides_hint: Use this when a node's Docker hostname doesn't resolve from inside the OmniGrid container. Keys are hostnames; values are full URLs. Overrides take precedence over the template.
host_stats.overrides_invalid: Per-node overrides must be a JSON object like {"host": "http://…"}.
host_stats.beszel_url_hint: e.g. http://beszel.example.com:8090 — the Hub's web UI address. No trailing slash.
host_stats.beszel_required: Beszel Hub URL and identity are required.
host_stats.beszel_password_required: Password is required the first time you configure Beszel.
host_stats.beszel_naming_hint: Match each Beszel system's Name to the Docker hostname shown in the Nodes view. Mismatched names produce a per-node 'no system named …' error.
host_stats.pulse_url_hint_html: Base URL of your Pulse instance. OmniGrid will call {URL_PLACEHOLDER}/api/state with the token below.
host_stats.pulse_token_placeholder: Create a read-only token in Pulse → Settings
host_stats.pulse_aliases_hint: Per-node aliases (Docker hostname → PVE node name) are edited from the Nodes view; Pulse and Beszel keep independent maps since their naming conventions diverge.
host_stats.webmin_user_hint_html: A dedicated read-only Webmin user. See notes/notes_agent_research.txt for the recommended ACL + no_session directive.
host_stats.webmin_verify_tls_label: Verify TLS certificate (Webmin's default cert is self-signed — leave off for homelab)
host_stats.webmin_aliases_hint_html: Per-host Webmin URLs are configured in Admin → Hosts — add a webmin_name + webmin_url on any row whose box runs Webmin. Hosts without a mapping are silently skipped by the Webmin probe.

ping.section_desc: Reachability + RTT only — no agents, no credentials. TCP-connect (works without privileges) is the default; raw ICMP is opt-in and requires the icmplib package + CAP_NET_RAW. Per-host opt-in: each host you want to ping must have its checkbox flipped in Admin → Hosts.
ping.default_port_hint: Used when a host doesn't override the port. Common picks: 443 (HTTPS), 22 (SSH), 80 (HTTP), 53 (DNS), 7 (echo). Hosts that respond on this port show clean RTT; hosts whose port is closed but reachable show alive=true with the connect-fail latency.
ping.use_icmp_label: Prefer raw ICMP echo when possible
ping.use_icmp_hint: Switches the global default transport to ICMP. Requires the icmplib Python package AND CAP_NET_RAW on the container. Falls back to TCP transparently if either is missing. Per-host transport overrides win.
ping.icmp_unavailable: icmplib package not installed — TCP-connect probes only.
ping.test_no_hosts: No hosts have ping enabled yet. Flip the checkbox in Admin → Hosts on at least one row.

snmp.section_desc: Sixth host-stats provider. Reads CPU / memory / disk / uptime / interface counters from devices that speak SNMP v2c / v3 USM but not unix-style agents — managed switches, routers, UPSes, network printers, managed APs. Per-host probe (no central hub): each curated row maps to one SNMP target. Per-host overrides (community / version / port / v3 keys) live on Admin → Hosts; the values below are the global defaults.
snmp.unavailable: pysnmp package not installed — SNMP probes are disabled until the dep is added to requirements.txt and the container restarts.
snmp.community_hint: Read-only community string for SNMP v2c. Most home-lab gear uses 'public' out of the box; rotate to a custom value on the device side and mirror it here. Per-host override on Admin → Hosts wins per row.
snmp.v3_section_hint: Used when the default version above is v3. Auth/priv key combinations: leave both blank for noAuthNoPriv, fill auth only for authNoPriv (HMAC-SHA-256), fill both for authPriv (HMAC-SHA-256 + AES-128).
snmp.aliases_label: Per-host alias map (Docker hostname → SNMP target)
snmp.aliases_hint: Optional JSON object mapping a curated host's id to its SNMP-reachable address. Useful when the bare hostname doesn't resolve from the container or the device is on a different management VLAN. Rows without an alias use the curated row's snmp_name (Admin → Hosts) and finally the bare id.
snmp.aliases_invalid: SNMP aliases must be a JSON object like {"host": "192.X.X.10"}.
snmp.version_invalid: SNMP default version must be 'v2c' or 'v3'.
snmp.test_host_required: Enter a host or IP to test against.

language.title: Language & region
language.description: Choose the interface language. Direction (LTR/RTL) follows the language automatically. Additional language files can be dropped into the static/i18n/ directory.
language.label: Language

profile.username_hint: Set by admin — can't be changed here.
profile.username_hint_authentik: Set by Authentik — can't be changed here.
profile.email_hint_authentik: Managed by Authentik — can't be changed here.
profile.display_name_placeholder: How you'd like your name shown (optional)
profile.bio_placeholder: A short description, role within the team, timezone — whatever helps others recognise you.
profile.password_hint: Changing your password signs out every other session for your account. The session you're using right now stays logged in.
profile.authentik_hint: Your account is managed by Authentik. Change your password there — OmniGrid doesn't store one for SSO users.
profile.api_token_hint: API-token sessions can't change a password. Sign in with the underlying user account to manage credentials.

totp.title: Two-factor authentication
totp.intro: Add a 6-digit code from your authenticator app (Aegis / Authy / 1Password / Bitwarden / Google Authenticator) on top of your password.
totp.disabled_by_admin: Two-factor authentication is currently disabled by an admin policy.
totp.authentik_hint: Authentik manages your second factor. Enable / disable it in your Authentik account settings.
totp.not_enabled_status: Two-factor authentication is not enabled for your account.
totp.qr_hint: Scan this QR with your authenticator app, then type the generated code below to confirm.
totp.reveal_warning: Each code can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator app. Store them somewhere safe — you won't see them masked here.
totp.enabled_status: Two-factor authentication is active for your account.
totp.backup_codes_hint: Single-use codes for when your authenticator app isn't available. Used codes are crossed out.
totp.regen_confirm_body: Any unused codes will be discarded. You'll be shown a fresh batch of 10.
totp.disable_blocked_title: Admin policy requires 2FA for your role.
totp.disable_confirm_body: Your account will be protected by password only. Re-confirm with your current password to continue.

passkeys.title: Passkeys (security keys)
passkeys.subtitle: Hardware keys (YubiKey, Titan, Solo), platform authenticators (Touch ID, Windows Hello, Android fingerprint), and password managers (1Password, iCloud Keychain, Bitwarden) can sign you in without typing a code.
passkeys.browser_unsupported: This browser doesn't support passkeys. Try Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge.
passkeys.server_unsupported: Passkey support isn't installed on this server.
passkeys.name_prompt_body: Pick a label so you can identify it later (e.g. "YubiKey 5C", "iPhone Touch ID", "1Password vault").
passkeys.revoke_confirm_body: {name} will stop working immediately. You can always add it again later.
passkeys.registering_hint: Confirm on your device — touch the key, allow the prompt, or unlock with biometrics.
passkeys.disabled_by_admin: Passkey enrolment is disabled by an administrator. Existing keys keep working until you revoke them OR the toggle goes back on.
passkeys.orphaned_title: This passkey was registered under {rp_id} and won't work on the current domain. Revoke and re-enrol it here.

notifications.title: Apprise notifications
notifications.url_format_suffix: is the bundle name you saved in the Apprise web UI (not a tag). A 404 response means no config with that key exists on the server.
notifications.tag_placeholder: Optional: filter URLs by tag within the config

portainer.connection_desc: URL + API key + endpoint ID that OmniGrid talks to. Stored in the database; changes take effect on the next refresh.
portainer.api_key_placeholder: Paste new key or leave blank
portainer.api_key_hint: Create in Portainer → My account → Access tokens. We store only the value itself; nothing is echoed back to the browser.
portainer.public_desc: Browser-facing URL for "Open in Portainer" deep links. May differ from the API URL when Portainer sits behind a reverse proxy.

ignores.title: Ignore list
ignores.intro_prefix: Items matching these patterns show as

oidc.title: Authentik OIDC (SSO)
oidc.description: OpenID Connect provider. Works with any compliant IdP; the label says Authentik because that's our reference setup.
oidc.enable_toggle: Enable OIDC login (shows the "Sign in with Authentik" button on /login)
oidc.issuer_hint: Paste the "OpenID Configuration Issuer" URL from your Authentik provider's Metadata tab. We append /.well-known/openid-configuration to it automatically.
oidc.client_secret_placeholder: Paste new secret or leave blank
oidc.redirect_uri_note: (paste this into Authentik's Redirect URIs allowlist)
oidc.redirect_uri_hint: Computed from the current request's origin. Authentik must have this exact value in its "Redirect URIs" allowlist, one per line.
oidc.scopes_hint: Space-separated. groups is required for role mapping.
oidc.admin_group_hint: Group membership is re-checked on every OIDC login. Users outside this group land as readonly.
oidc.verify_tls_title: Verify TLS on discovery / token / JWKS calls
oidc.verify_tls_hint: Leave on when the issuer has a publicly-trusted cert. Turn off for homelab issuers behind an internal CA whose root isn't in the container's trust store (e.g. *.example.com with a self-signed PKI). Mirrors the behaviour of Portainer's Verify TLS toggle.
oidc.group_case_sensitive_hint: Leave on (default) for byte-exact matching of the admin group claim. Turn off when the IdP returns mixed-case group names that don't match the value you typed above (e.g. you typed OmniGrid-Admins but Authentik returns omnigrid-admins). Off compares both sides lowered.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Press <kbd class="kbd">?</kbd> anywhere to open the full reference.

admin.intro: User accounts, active sessions, and API tokens. Destructive actions prompt for confirmation.

nodes.prune_prompt_html:
Run docker system prune -af --volumes on {host}?

This deletes all stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and unreferenced volumes. Active services are not affected.

general.title: General
general.subtitle: UI-side service settings that don't fit auth, hosts, scheduling, or notifications. Today: Open-Meteo (topbar weather widget). Future home for any other ambient/UI integrations.

events.title: Per-event notifications
events.subtitle: Enable / disable specific notification events. Test notifications always send regardless of these toggles.
events.test_always_sends: Test notifications always send regardless of these toggles.

config.title: Process tunables
config.subtitle: Override the {count} runtime knobs that would otherwise require an env-var change + restart. Each value resolves via DB setting → env var → code default. Leave a field blank to clear the override and fall back to the env / default shown in the placeholder. UI changes take effect on the next consumer read — TTLs immediate, samplers on the next tick.

bounds.default_title: Default value (used when both the DB override and the env var are blank)

errors.must_be_int: {field} must be a whole number (no decimals).

tuning_cache_ttl_seconds.label: Items cache TTL (seconds)
tuning_cache_ttl_seconds.help: How long the /api/items result (services + containers + stacks + remote registry digests) stays valid before the next caller triggers a fresh _gather() pass. Drives the Stacks / Services tables. Lower it if you want "check for new image digests" to feel more live (cost: every call past TTL pays the full registry-digest sweep, ~5-30s on a fleet with many private images). Raise it to lighten outbound load on Docker Hub / private registries when you don't need fresh digest data more than once every N minutes. Default 900s (15 min). The SPA's force-refresh hotkey (Shift+R) and the auto-refresh ticker bypass this cache via ?force=true.

tuning_stats_cache_ttl_seconds.label: Stats cache TTL (seconds)
tuning_stats_cache_ttl_seconds.help: How long the /api/stats result (per-container CPU% / memory / image disk size) stays valid before the next caller triggers a fresh stats poll. This is a SEPARATE cache from items so the SPA's 15s stats-poll doesn't keep refreshing the slow registry-digest sweep. Lower it to make per-row CPU / Memory bars feel more live (cost: more parallel /containers/{id}/stats calls to the Docker daemon via Portainer). Raise it on a large fleet to cut Docker daemon load. Default 30s — matches the SPA's 30s polling interval so most polls are cache hits.

tuning_registry_concurrency.label: Registry digest concurrency
tuning_registry_concurrency.help: Maximum parallel HEAD requests OmniGrid fires at remote container registries (Docker Hub, ghcr, registry.k8s.io, your private registry) per _gather() pass to resolve each image's :tag → @sha256 digest. The semaphore caps the concurrent in-flight requests so a fleet with 100 unique images doesn't open 100 sockets at once. Lower it when a registry rate-limits you (Docker Hub anonymous pulls cap aggressively — symptom: 429 / 401 in [registry] log lines). Raise it on a beefy network with multiple registries so the digest pass finishes faster. Default 8.

tuning_stats_concurrency.label: Container stats concurrency
tuning_stats_concurrency.help: Maximum parallel /containers/{id}/stats?stream=false calls to the Docker daemon (via Portainer) during one stats gather. Each call costs ~50-200ms, so a fleet with 80 running containers can take 5+ seconds even at this concurrency. Lower it on a slow / loaded Swarm manager so the stats sweep doesn't compete with normal Docker scheduling. Raise it on a fast manager with many containers so the sweep finishes inside the SPA's 30s poll cadence. Default 16.

tuning_stats_history_days.label: Time-series retention (days)
tuning_stats_history_days.help: How long every time-series table (stats_samples, host_net_samples, host_metrics_samples) keeps rows before the hourly pruner deletes them. Drives the inline sparklines on the Stacks view + the Hosts drawer's CPU / Memory / Disk / Net history charts. Lower it when SQLite is growing too large on a small disk (each sample row is ~80 bytes, but a 60-host fleet sampled every 5min for 30d adds up). Raise it for longer-term graphs. Default 7d. Bumping this RETROACTIVELY surfaces older data immediately (no backfill — rows already exist, the cutoff just moves).

tuning_stats_sample_interval_seconds.label: Sampler interval (seconds)
tuning_stats_sample_interval_seconds.help: Cadence for every lifespan-managed sampler: container stats → stats_samples, node-exporter network counters → host_net_samples, full host metrics → host_metrics_samples. Lower it for finer-grained sparklines + faster response in the Net I/O fallback chart (cost: N× more rows in SQLite for the same retention window — re-tune `tuning_stats_history_days` together if you halve this). Raise it on a Pi / underpowered host to reduce sampler CPU. Default 300s (5 min). Takes effect on the NEXT tick after Save (current sleep finishes first).

tuning_host_permanent_fail_window_second.label: Host sampling pause window (seconds)
tuning_host_permanent_fail_window_second.help: After this many seconds of CONSECUTIVE probe failures (no successful sample landed in this window), the host_metrics_sampler stops sampling that host entirely — no more probe attempts, no more failure rows in the log. The host shows a paused badge in the Hosts table and an explanatory banner in the drawer. Resume manually via the drawer's "Resume sampling" button. Lower it to react faster to dead hosts (less log noise from a host that crashed); raise it to tolerate longer transient outages without auto-pausing (e.g. a host that reboots every Sunday for an hour). Default 900s (15 min).

tuning_ops_poll_interval_seconds.label: Ops poll cadence (seconds)
tuning_ops_poll_interval_seconds.help: How often the SPA polls /api/ops while it's open to detect when a background operation (stack update, container restart, prune, etc.) finishes. Drives the live progress chip + auto-refresh after success. When SSE is connected (Live mode), the cadence stretches to a 30s safety-net keepalive because op deltas arrive via push instead. Lower this (e.g. 1) for snappier feedback on a click → "Done" cycle (cost: more idle GET /api/ops requests when no ops are running); raise it (e.g. 5–10) to cut idle traffic on a long-open SPA tab. Default 2 seconds. Internally stored as seconds and multiplied × 1000 before being delivered to the SPA's setTimeout via `/api/me`'s `client_config.ops_poll_ms`. Takes effect on the next /api/me round-trip.

tuning_log_retention_days.label: Log retention (days)
tuning_log_retention_days.help: How long persistent log files at /app/data/logs/omnigrid-YYYY-MM-DD.log are kept before the hourly pruner sweep deletes them. The in-memory ring buffer in Admin → Logs (Live tab) is INDEPENDENT — it always shows the last 2000 lines from the current process and is unaffected by this setting. Lower it if disk space is tight; raise it for longer audit trails / forensic windows. Default 7d. Pruner clamps to (1, 365) at consume time so a corrupt DB row can't disable the sweep entirely.

tuning_host_snapshots_cache_ttl_seconds.label: Host snapshots cache TTL (seconds)
tuning_host_snapshots_cache_ttl_seconds.help: Short-TTL read cache for the host_snapshots SQLite table — the source of stale-fallback data when a provider goes offline mid-session. The SPA fans out N parallel /api/hosts/one/{id} calls per refresh, each of which would otherwise issue its own SELECT against host_snapshots; caching collapses N reads into 1 per TTL window. Cache is auto-busted on every snapshot WRITE so write→read consistency is immediate (no stale-data risk). Lower it / set to 0 to disable the cache entirely (every call hits the DB — only useful for debugging cache-related stale-data bugs). Raise it on a large fleet where the parallel SELECT stampede shows up in profiling. Default 5s.

tuning_hosts_parallel_fetch.label: Hosts fan-out concurrency
tuning_hosts_parallel_fetch.help: Cap on the SPA's PARALLEL /api/hosts/one/{id} calls during a Hosts-tab load / refresh. The SPA fetches the curated host list first (fast skeleton), then fans out per-host probes with this concurrency. Lower it if a slow Webmin / unreachable node-exporter saturates NPM's upstream pool — symptom is 504s on UNRELATED static-asset requests (icons, fonts) because their requests queue behind the slow per-host probes. Raise it on a beefy NPM + manager with many hosts so the Hosts tab paints fully in fewer wall-clock seconds. Default 6. Takes effect on the next /api/me round-trip.

tuning_sse_heartbeat_seconds.label: SSE heartbeat cadence (seconds)
tuning_sse_heartbeat_seconds.help: How often the /api/events stream emits a `: keepalive` comment so an idle reverse proxy (NPM / Cloudflare / Traefik) doesn't drop the connection on its own idle-keepalive timer. Lower this if your proxy has a tight idle timeout (some defaults are 30s); raise it to cut comment-traffic on long-lived tabs. Default 25 seconds. Takes effect on the NEXT SSE reconnect — already-open connections keep their old cadence until they cycle (every 6h via the lifetime cap, OR after a network drop).

tuning_sse_max_lifetime_seconds.label: SSE connection lifetime cap (seconds)
tuning_sse_max_lifetime_seconds.help: Forces a periodic close + reconnect on each /api/events stream so the cookie-authed tab re-enters the auth middleware, letting the session-cookie's sliding-window refresh land before the 8-hour session hard cap expires. Default 21600 (6 hours), leaving a 1-hour margin for clock skew + heartbeat round-trip on flaky networks. Do NOT raise past 25200 (7 hours) — the upper bound exists to keep the safety margin against the session hard cap. Lower this aggressively (e.g. 3600 = 1 hour) on networks with frequent NAT timeouts; the SPA's EventSource auto-reconnects on the synthetic close, so the operator never sees the cycle.

tuning_webmin_probe_budget_seconds.label: Webmin probe budget (seconds)
tuning_webmin_probe_budget_seconds.help: Outer wall-clock budget for the per-host Webmin probe (system-status + package-updates + mount + net via Miniserv's three-tier XML/JSON/HTML fallback). Caps how long ONE host's Webmin call can block. Used by both the legacy /api/hosts (fleet-wide) AND the per-host /api/hosts/one paths so they stay in lockstep. Lower it (e.g. 10s) on a fleet of fast Miniservs to surface failures faster; raise it for fleets with slow Webmin instances that need the full XML→JSON→HTML fallback chain. Default 20 seconds. Pairs with the 5s negative-cache TTL so a hung Webmin doesn't burn 20s × N parallel calls.

tuning_node_exporter_probe_timeout_secon.label: node-exporter probe timeout (seconds)
tuning_node_exporter_probe_timeout_secon.help: HTTP timeout on every node-exporter scrape, applied uniformly across the legacy /api/hosts fleet probe, the per-host /api/hosts/one probe, the /api/hosts/debug endpoint, AND the lifespan host_metrics_sampler. Lower it on a network where node-exporter usually responds in milliseconds and you want fast-fail on broken hosts; raise it for nodes with deliberately slow exporters or saturated metrics endpoints. Default 10 seconds. Pre-#540 the sampler used 15s while the request-path used 10s — drift that's now closed.

tuning_sse_idle_threshold_seconds.label: SSE freshness threshold (seconds)
tuning_sse_idle_threshold_seconds.help: How long the SPA waits without seeing ANY event (organic or heartbeat) before flipping the live-mode badge to 'polling fallback'. Defence-in-depth on top of EventSource's onerror, which doesn't always fire on silent half-open sockets. Lower it for faster fallback detection on flaky networks (cost: false positives during normal heartbeat gaps); raise it to suppress brief stalls. Default 30 seconds — matches the heartbeat cadence so a stalled stream missing both heartbeats AND organic events triggers fallback within ~2 heartbeat windows. Stored as seconds; SPA multiplies × 1000 for its setInterval comparison.

tuning_pollops_sse_keepalive_seconds.label: Ops poll keepalive when SSE is up (seconds)
tuning_pollops_sse_keepalive_seconds.help: When SSE is healthy, op deltas arrive via push (the `op:created/updated/completed` events), so /api/ops doesn't need fast polling. The SPA's pollOps loop slows from `tuning_ops_poll_interval_seconds` (the fast cadence) to THIS value as a safety-net keepalive — catches a silently-stalled stream that the freshness watchdog hasn't yet detected. Default 30 seconds, lined up with the freshness threshold so the keepalive fires at-or-before the watchdog flips _sseConnected to false. Lower this if you want belt-and-braces faster recovery; raise it to cut idle traffic when SSE is reliable. Stored as seconds; SPA multiplies × 1000 for setTimeout.

tuning_rate_limit_max_failures.label: Login rate-limit failures threshold
tuning_rate_limit_max_failures.help: Maximum consecutive failed login attempts (per IP, AND independently per IP+username pair) before the account locks out. Default 5 — matches OWASP's recommendation for interactive logins. Lower for high-security deployments (e.g. 3); raise for shared-IP environments where multiple operators may collide on bad-typed-passwords (e.g. 10). Resolved per failed-login attempt so a Save in Admin → Config takes effect immediately.

tuning_rate_limit_window_seconds.label: Login rate-limit window (seconds)
tuning_rate_limit_window_seconds.help: Sliding window over which failed login attempts are counted toward the threshold. After N seconds with no further failures the counter resets. Default 900 (15 minutes) — a long enough window to thwart slow-burn brute-force attempts but short enough that a one-off typo from yesterday doesn't carry over. Range 60s (very tight; aggressive recovery) to 86400s (24h; very strict). Resolved per failed-login attempt.

tuning_rate_limit_lockout_seconds.label: Login lockout duration (seconds)
tuning_rate_limit_lockout_seconds.help: Once the failure threshold is hit, how long the IP / IP+username bucket stays locked. Pre-lockout-expiry, every login attempt is rejected without checking the password. Default 900 (15 minutes). Lower for less-paranoid environments where operator typos shouldn't cost much wall-clock; raise (e.g. 3600 = 1 hour, or 86400 = 24h) for high-security deployments where a brute-force attempt should cost the attacker meaningfully. Range 60s-86400s. Resolved per blocked-login check.

tuning_host_provider_cache_ttl_seconds.label: Host-provider cache TTL (seconds)
tuning_host_provider_cache_ttl_seconds.help: How long the batch-provider state (Beszel hub map + Pulse hub map + Webmin creds + active-sources tuple) is cached before the next call re-probes. Settings saves explicitly invalidate this cache so the matter only when an UPSTREAM change happens silently (e.g. a new host appears in Beszel without a settings save). Default 10 seconds. Lower for live-feeling drawer reopens after upstream changes; raise to cut hub probe load on small fleets where the data is mostly static.

tuning_webmin_host_cache_ttl_seconds.label: Webmin per-host success cache TTL (seconds)
tuning_webmin_host_cache_ttl_seconds.help: How long a SUCCESSFUL Webmin probe result is cached per host before the next call re-probes. Burst refreshes (SPA fan-out, drawer reopens) within the window skip the probe entirely and reuse the last known-good stats. Default 30 seconds. Lower for live-feeling drawer reopens; raise to cut Miniserv load on a fleet where most data is stable. Pairs with the failure-cache TTL — a fresh failure on a previously-cached success drops the success entry immediately, so this knob doesn't affect failure-detection latency.

tuning_webmin_host_fail_cache_ttl_second.label: Webmin per-host failure cache TTL (seconds)
tuning_webmin_host_fail_cache_ttl_second.help: How long a FAILED Webmin probe result is cached per host so a SPA fan-out burst doesn't burn 20 seconds × N parallel calls on an unreachable host. Default 5 seconds — tight enough that recovery is felt within one Hosts-tab refresh cycle, long enough to dedupe a fan-out burst. Operators with constantly-flapping Webmin instances may want 30s+ to suppress the spam; operators wanting fastest possible recovery detection can drop to 1s.

tuning_host_metrics_probe_concurrency.label: Host metrics sampler concurrency
tuning_host_metrics_probe_concurrency.help: Maximum parallel node-exporter scrapes the lifespan host_metrics_sampler runs per tick. Default 8 — comfortable on a 60-host fleet through 8 workers in ~3 batches without saturating the manager. Lower on a Pi-class manager where 8 parallel HTTP scrapes saturate the network or CPU; raise on a beefy host or a fleet of many hosts where serialised batches push past the 5-minute interval.

tuning_auth_failure_cooldown_seconds.label: Auth-failure cool-down (seconds)
tuning_auth_failure_cooldown_seconds.help: Per-(host, user) cool-down applied after a Webmin or SSH probe gets a 401. During the cool-down, OmniGrid skips that host's probe entirely (returning the cached failure) instead of re-burning a doomed call. Shared between Webmin and SSH so a single Save propagates to both. Default 300 seconds (5 minutes) — long enough to avoid lockout cascades on bad credentials, short enough that operators don't have to wait an hour after fixing a typo.

tuning_ping_interval_seconds.label: Ping sampler interval (seconds)
tuning_ping_interval_seconds.help: Cadence at which the lifespan ping_sampler walks every opted-in host and writes a ping_samples row. Lower for finer-grained latency charts (cost: more wire-level traffic AND a denser SQLite table); raise to cut sampling cost on large fleets. Default 60 seconds — same scale as the metrics sampler. Range 10s..3600s.

tuning_ping_concurrency.label: Ping probe concurrency
tuning_ping_concurrency.help: Maximum parallel ping probes the sampler runs in one tick. Default 16 — fits a 60-host fleet through 16 workers in ~4 sequential batches. Lower if probes are saturating the network's outbound concurrency budget (NPM, ISP modem, etc.); raise on a beefy host with many opted-in hosts to keep the per-tick wall-clock under the interval. Range 1..128.

tuning_ping_probe_timeout_seconds.label: Ping per-probe timeout (seconds)
tuning_ping_probe_timeout_seconds.help: Wall-clock budget for ONE TCP-connect attempt (or one ICMP echo round-trip). Each tick fires 3 probes per host; this knob caps each one independently. Default 2 seconds — enough for cross-WAN latencies but tight enough to not stall the sampler on dead hosts. Lower if your fleet is all on-LAN (sub-millisecond RTTs); raise if you're probing hosts over a satellite link or a flaky upstream. Range 1..30.

tuning_ping_cooldown_seconds.label: Ping cool-down on consecutive timeouts (seconds)
tuning_ping_cooldown_seconds.help: After TWO consecutive timeout-batches against the same host:port, the probe arms a cool-down — subsequent ticks skip that pair until the cool-down expires. Cuts wire traffic against permanently-unreachable hosts without removing them from the chart entirely. Default 300 seconds (5 minutes). Lower for fast-recovery detection; raise to suppress probes against a host that's been down for hours. Range 30..3600.

tuning_snmp_probe_timeout_seconds.label: SNMP per-probe timeout (seconds)
tuning_snmp_probe_timeout_seconds.help: Wall-clock budget for ONE SNMP GET / GETBULK round-trip. The probe fires a sysName/sysDescr/sysUpTime GET plus several walks (hrStorage / hrProcessorLoad / ifTable) in parallel under this budget. Default 5 seconds — enough for slow embedded devices to ack on UDP without stalling the gather pipeline on a dead host. Lower if your fleet is purely on-LAN with < 50 ms RTTs; raise for cross-WAN devices or chatty UPS units that are slow to respond. Range 1..60.

tuning_snmp_concurrency.label: SNMP probe concurrency
tuning_snmp_concurrency.help: Maximum parallel SNMP probes the gather + per-host-merge paths run in one tick. Default 16 — comfortable on a 50-host SNMP fleet through 16 workers in ~3 batches. Lower if your network gear's CPU is fragile (some older switches lock up under > 8 parallel SNMP requests); raise on a beefy fleet of well-provisioned UPSes / routers. Range 1..128.

totp.title: Two-factor authentication (TOTP)
totp.subtitle: Policy controls for local-auth users. Authentik users skip every TOTP path — their IdP handles MFA. Each enrolled user keeps their own QR / backup codes; admins can disable a user's 2FA from the Users tab when a device is lost.
totp.allowed_hint: Master switch. When off, TOTP enrolment is hidden from Profile AND existing TOTP-enrolled users are NOT prompted for a code at login. Their secret stays in the DB for when you flip it back on.
totp.passkeys_allowed_hint: Master switch for WebAuthn / FIDO2 passkeys. When off, the passkey "Add" button hides in Profile AND already-enrolled passkeys are NOT offered at login. Existing rows stay in the DB so the same user can revoke them, and login resumes when you flip it back on.
totp.required_hint: Forced enrolment runs at login: a user without 2FA gets the QR + verification step before the cookie is issued.

debug.title: Debug & diagnostics
debug.intro: Admin-wide switches for the "show me internals" affordances scattered across the SPA. Every toggle here persists on change — no Save button.

logs.title: Application logs
logs.clear_prompt_text: This empties the buffer that powers this viewer. Docker logs on the host are unaffected.
logs.empty: No log lines captured yet — activity here mirrors stdout/stderr in real time.
logs.hint: In-memory ring buffer of recent stdout/stderr (capped at 2000 lines). Tees from real streams, so Docker logs still see everything.
logs.copy_title: Copy the currently visible (filtered) log lines to the clipboard as plain text.
logs.files_empty: No log files on disk yet — first activity in this UTC day will create one.

schedules.create_hint: Admin-defined recurring jobs. Each tick checks once a minute; schedules fire when their interval elapses. Runs go through the regular ops system, so they appear in the live ops panel and the history table with actor = scheduler.
schedules.params_must_be_object: Params must be a JSON object.
schedules.interval_too_small: Interval must be at least {min} seconds.

errors.prune_logs_days_range: Params 'days' must be an integer between 1 and 365.

schedules.queue_empty: No scheduled runs yet. They'll appear here once a schedule fires.
schedules.empty: No schedules yet. Create one above.
schedules.delete_prompt_text: This stops '{name}' from firing. In-flight runs are not affected.
schedules.run_prompt_destructive_html: This kind ('{kind}') is destructive. It will run against the configured target immediately, regardless of the schedule's interval.
schedules.run_at_placeholder: HH:MM — leave empty to use interval
schedules.run_at_hint: When set, fires once daily at this local clock time and ignores the interval.
schedules.cadence_invalid: Cadence mode must be interval, daily, weekly, or monthly.
schedules.weekly_needs_days: Weekly schedule needs at least one selected day.

toasts.run_started: '{name}' queued — watch the ops panel for progress

users.authentik_hint: Authentik users are usually auto-provisioned on first SSO login — create manually only if you want to pre-set their role.
users.totp_disable_title: Clear this user's TOTP enrolment (use when they've lost their authenticator device).
users.totp_disable_confirm_body: Their stored secret + backup codes will be erased. They re-enrol on next login if policy still requires 2FA.
users.totp_required_title: 2FA is required for this user (per-user override). They will be forced to enrol on next login.
users.totp_force_title: Require this user to set up 2FA regardless of the global policy.
users.totp_unforce_title: Drop the per-user 2FA requirement. Global policy still applies.
users.totp_force_confirm_body: This user will be forced to enrol in 2FA on their next login, even if the global policy doesn't require it for their role.
users.totp_unforce_confirm_body: Their existing 2FA stays enrolled (use Disable 2FA if you also want to clear it). The global role policy still applies.

sessions.hint: Bearer-token calls are not shown here — revoke those from the API tokens tab.

tokens.create_hint: For machine clients (Homarr widgets, scheduled jobs, etc.). The raw token is shown ONCE after creation — copy it then.

token_modal.title: New API token — copy it now
token_modal.hint: This is the ONLY time we'll show this value. We store a SHA-256 hash and can't recover the raw token later.
token_modal.name: Name

backups.create_desc: A zip containing the full SQLite database (users, API tokens, ignores, settings including Portainer + OIDC config, history, stats) and every uploaded avatar. Consistent snapshot via SQLite's online .backup() API — safe to run with active traffic.
backups.restore_prompt_html: This OVERWRITES the current database and avatars. A safety snapshot of the current state will be taken first — look for auto-before-restore-* in the list afterwards.
backups.restore_upload_html:
{name} ({size} MB)

This OVERWRITES the current database and avatars. A safety snapshot will be taken first.
backups.restore_complete_signout: You may need to sign out and back in.
backups.delete_user_confirm: This permanently removes {name} and all their sessions.
backups.revoke_session_text: Signs out {name} from this session immediately.
backups.revoke_token_text: Any client using "{name}" will start failing immediately.
backups.retention_hint: 0 = keep all. Applies after each create (manual or scheduled); older .zip files are deleted oldest-first.

drawer.external_warning: ⚠ This service has no associated Portainer stack (external / CLI-deployed).

hotkeys.title: Keyboard shortcuts
hotkeys.hint: Shortcuts are disabled while typing in an input field. Press <kbd class="kbd">Esc</kbd> to exit a field.

toasts.copy_failed_manual: Copy failed — select and copy manually
toasts.password_changed: Password changed. Other sessions were signed out.
toasts.authentik_change_pw_here: Authentik users change their password in Authentik
toasts.totp_forced_on: 2FA is now required for this user
toasts.totp_force_failed: Could not change the per-user 2FA requirement
toasts.totp_required_no_disable: Admin policy requires 2FA for your role
toasts.passkey_server_unsupported: Passkey support isn't installed on this server

dialogs.update_stack_html:
{name}
  • Prune removed services
  • Re-pull all images
  • Redeploy the stack
dialogs.recreate_container_html:
{name}
Pull the latest image and recreate the container.
dialogs.restart_service_html:
{name}
Force an update with the same image — running tasks will be replaced. No image pull.
dialogs.remove_container_html:
{name}
Stops and deletes this container (force + volumes). Cannot be undone.
dialogs.bulk_remove_html:
Stops and deletes the following stopped container(s) (force + volumes). Cannot be undone.
    {items}{more}

login.title: Sign in to continue.
login.too_many_attempts: Too many attempts. Try again shortly.
login.account_disabled: Account is disabled. Contact your administrator.
login.totp_required_hint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
login.totp_backup_hint: Enter one of your saved backup codes.
login.totp_locked: Account locked due to too many failed 2FA attempts.
login.totp_setup_hint: Two-factor authentication is required. Scan the QR with your authenticator app, then enter the generated code below.
login.totp_backup_warning: Store these somewhere safe. Each code can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator app.
login.passkey_or_code_hint: Tap a passkey, or type a code below.
login.passkey_only_hint: Sign in with the passkey you registered.
login.passkey_failed: Passkey sign-in failed. Try again or use a code.
login.passkey_rp_id_mismatch: Your passkeys were registered under a different domain and can't be used here. Sign in another way (TOTP / password recovery), then re-enrol them from Profile → Security on the new domain ({current}). Orphaned: {orphans}.

new_token_modal.title: New API token — copy it now
new_token_modal.hint: This is the ONLY time we'll show this value. We store a SHA-256 hash and can't recover the raw token later.

topbar_extra.new_version_title: New version {version} deployed — click to reload and use the updated UI

permanent_fail.badge_title: Sampling paused — host has been unreachable for {minutes}m
permanent_fail.drawer_banner: Sampling paused: this host has been unreachable for {minutes} min. Last error: {error}
permanent_fail.last_error_age_seconds: Last probe attempt {seconds}s ago — not yet retried since pause.
permanent_fail.last_error_age_minutes: Last probe attempt {minutes}m ago — not yet retried since pause.
permanent_fail.last_error_age_hours: Last probe attempt {hours}h ago — not yet retried since pause.

hosts_extra.sort_title: Sort hosts — persisted in this browser
hosts_extra.hide_unconfigured_title: Hide curated rows that have no Beszel / Pulse / node-exporter / Webmin field set — inventory-only entries are filtered out. Persisted in this browser.

no_data.title: No data from any enabled provider
no_data.body: OmniGrid could not match this host to Pulse, Beszel, node-exporter, or Webmin. Open the {debug_label} panel below to see what each provider returned and where the match failed.

hosts_extra.provider_failing: {provider} is enabled and mapped for this host but returned no data (probe failed / agent paused / unreachable / auth error). Open the drawer's Debug panel for the provider's raw response.

net_inactive.title: No NIC activity reported
net_inactive.body_in: The Beszel agent on this host isn't tracking any NIC. Add {code} (or your NIC name from {ipcmd}) to the agent's env, then restart it.
net_inactive.body_in_ne: No traffic on this host's physical NICs in the selected window. node-exporter excludes loopback, Docker bridges, veth pairs, and other virtual interfaces from totals — a host whose only activity is on those won't register here.
net_inactive.body_out: Add {code} to the Beszel agent's env (see Net In card for details).

collectors_missing.disk_io_body: node-exporter on this host isn't exposing {metric}. Enable the diskstats collector (e.g. {flag}) and restart node-exporter to populate this chart.
collectors_missing.net_body: node-exporter on this host isn't exposing {metric}. Enable the netstats collector (e.g. {flag}) and restart node-exporter to populate this chart.

hosts_extra.custom_number_title: Operator-assigned catalogue number — used for sort/group in Hosts view and as the key for Asset-inventory lookups
hosts_extra.refresh_title: Force refresh now — normally polls every 15s.
hosts_extra.open_beszel_title: Open the Beszel hub in a new tab.
hosts_extra.open_pulse_title: Open Pulse in a new tab.

metrics.disk_io_idle: No disk I/O activity in this window
metrics.source_temperature: Per-sensor thermal readings (°C). Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.t — a {sensor: celsius} dict). Hosts whose agent doesn't expose any thermal sensor (most VMs, some hardware lacking a hwmon driver) hide this chart entirely. Chart line is the peak across all sensors at each tick; the live header lists every sensor with its current reading.
metrics.source_cpu: CPU usage (0–100%). Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.cpu) when configured. The node-exporter sampler doesn't track CPU yet, so NE-only hosts show no data here.
metrics.source_memory: Memory usage. Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.mp / mu) for Beszel-monitored hosts; node-exporter (host_mem_used / host_mem_total from host_metrics_samples) for NE-only hosts.
metrics.source_disk: Root-filesystem usage. Source: Beszel (system_stats.dp) or node-exporter (host_disk_used / host_disk_total).
metrics.source_network: Per-NIC throughput, excluding loopback / Docker bridges / veth pairs. Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.nr / .ns) when the agent has NICS=eth0 configured. Falls back to node-exporter rates from host_net_samples when Beszel returns zeros AND the host has ne_url set. Pure node-exporter for NE-only hosts.
metrics.source_disk_io: Block-device read/write rates. Source: Beszel (system_stats.dr / .dw) or node-exporter (host_disk_read_bps / host_disk_write_bps from host_metrics_samples). Excludes loop / ram / fd / sr / zram devices; keeps dm-* / md* (NAS volumes).
metrics.source_load_avg: 1m / 5m / 15m load averages. Source: Beszel agent only (system_stats.la). Not surfaced by the node-exporter sampler today.
metrics.source_bandwidth: Combined bandwidth (rx + tx). Same source as Network — Beszel or node-exporter.
metrics.source_swap: Swap usage % and used GiB. Source: Beszel agent only (system_stats.s / .su). Not surfaced by the node-exporter sampler today.
metrics.ping_loss_title: Packet loss across the polled window
metrics.source_ping: ICMP round-trip latency in milliseconds. Source: OmniGrid's built-in ping sampler (host_ping_samples), opt-in per host via Admin → Hosts → ping_enabled. Hosts without the toggle hide this chart entirely.

empty.no_match: No hosts match the current filter.
empty.all_disabled_html:
{count} host(s) defined but all disabled.
Open Admin → Hosts and tick Enabled on the rows you want to see here.
empty.no_provider_match_html:
{count} enabled host(s), but no provider returned data for any of them.
Check the per-row Test providers button in Admin → Hosts — the mapped provider names probably don't match what Beszel / Pulse / node-exporter actually report.
empty.no_provider_enabled_html:
No host-stats provider enabled.
Enable Beszel / Pulse / node-exporter in Admin → Host stats, then define the hosts you want to monitor in Admin → Hosts.
empty.no_data_html:
No host data to show.
If you've defined hosts in Admin → Hosts, the configured provider names probably don't match what {sources} are reporting — use the per-row Test providers button to see what each provider knows about, then fix the mapping.

hosts_extra.filter_placeholder: Filter hosts by name, platform, OS, provider…

stale_marker.tooltip: Last live data {age} ago — value restored from cache snapshot
stale_marker.never: No live data has been seen for this field yet.

stale.title: Showing cached data — provider unavailable
stale.body: Last live update {age} ago. {count} field(s) restored from the snapshot — values dimmed below are not live.

hardware.heading: Hardware
hardware.beszel_agent_title: Version reported by the Beszel agent on this host
hardware.load_title: Average CPU saturation over the last 1, 5, and 15 minutes (load ÷ cores × 100). 100% means the run-queue averaged one runnable process per core; >100% means processes were waiting for CPU.
hardware.asset_title: Sourced from asset inventory (matched by Custom #)

asset.heading: Asset inventory
asset.edit_title: Open this asset's edit page on the upstream in a new tab
asset.name: Name

network.heading: Network
network.docker_toggle_title: Toggle Docker veth pairs, bridges, and other internal interfaces (lo, docker0, br-*, veth*, cni*, flannel*, cali*, kube-*, vxlan*, vmbr*)

debug_panel.hint: — compare raw providers vs merged vs rendered

topbar_widgets.title: Topbar widgets
topbar_widgets.intro_prefix: Clock and weather appear in the top-right of the app bar. Weather uses
topbar_widgets.intro_suffix: (free, no API key, refreshed every 10 min).
topbar_widgets.tip_suffix: , right-click your city, copy the lat/lon pair shown at the top of the menu.
topbar_widgets.auto_save_hint: Changes also auto-save when you toggle or edit a field above.

scheduler_settings.title: Scheduler
scheduler_settings.intro: When a schedule says "Daily @ 01:00", which clock does "01:00" refer to? Leave blank to use the container's clock (typically UTC). Fill in to pin to your local wall time so "01:00" means 01:00 at your location.
scheduler_settings.tz_hint_suffix: . Invalid names fail the save with a 400.
scheduler_settings.saved_cleared: Scheduler timezone cleared (using container local time)
scheduler_settings.tz_fallback_warning: Configured timezone {tz} is not a valid IANA name — scheduler is falling back to container-local time. Edit the value above and Save to fix.

hosts_actions.unsaved_title: You have unsaved changes in the Hosts list.
hosts_actions.provider_blank_hint: Leave any provider field blank when that provider doesn't know this host — the row still populates from whatever else matches.
hosts_actions.host_stats_unsaved_title: You have unsaved changes in Host stats settings.
hosts_actions.collapse_all_title: Collapse every host row to its summary line

admin_integrations.open_meteo_desc: URL the topbar weather widget fetches from. Leave blank to use the public Open-Meteo endpoint; point at a self-hosted instance if you run one.
admin_integrations.open_meteo_hint: Blank = use the default public endpoint. Changes apply on the next 10-minute cache refresh.
admin_integrations.disabled_hint: Disabled — settings preserved, callers will short-circuit until re-enabled.

admin_hosts.heading: Hosts
admin_hosts.intro: Define the machines you want to monitor and map each to the names used by every enabled provider. OmniGrid fetches all providers on every refresh and merges the best values per host — missing fields degrade to what the other providers returned.
admin_hosts.label_source_asset: {name} — from asset inventory (no operator label set)
admin_hosts.discover_title: Fetch the list of names known to every enabled provider so you can autocomplete the fields below
admin_hosts.import_discovered_title: Create a row for every discovered name that isn't already configured. Duplicates are skipped.
admin_hosts.test_all_title: Probe every enabled host against all its configured providers and show the results per row.
admin_hosts.empty_html:
No hosts configured yet.
Click + Add host below to define one, or Discover from providers to pull every name the enabled providers know about.
admin_hosts.debug_panel_toggle_hint: When off, hides the per-host raw-JSON / merged-shape diagnostic panel for every admin.
admin_hosts.test_providers_disabled_title: Add a Beszel / Pulse / node-exporter / Webmin entry first

import.nothing_new: Nothing new to import — every discovered name is already configured.

test.no_eligible: No enabled hosts with provider mappings to test.

discover.no_results: No enabled provider returned any hosts — check connection settings

admin_hosts.added_n: Added {count} host(s) — review and Save to persist.
admin_hosts.imported_n: Imported {count} host(s) — review and Save to persist.
admin_hosts.import_replace_confirm_html: Replace all {existing} current hosts with {incoming} from the file?
admin_hosts.import_replace_confirm_merge: Merge (update existing IDs, add new ones)
admin_hosts.unsaved_confirm_html: You have unsaved changes in the Hosts list. Discard them and reload from the server?

errors.filtered_body: One or more rows have validation errors but live on rows hidden by the current filter. Clear the filter to reach them.

admin_hosts.webmin_test_url_required: Enter a Webmin URL to test.

autofill.button_title: Fill empty fields (hostname, label, URL, IP) from the matching asset-inventory record. Never overwrites values you've already typed.
autofill.nothing_to_fill: Asset matched, but every field you'd fill is already typed.

ssh_state.disabled: SSH disabled (operator override on this row)

row.duplicate_title: Duplicate this host (inserts a disabled clone below)

admin_hosts.filter_placeholder: Filter hosts by id, label, provider name, URL…
admin_hosts.export_title: Download the curated hosts list as a JSON file.
admin_hosts.import_title: Load a hosts JSON file — merge or replace the current list.

provider.webmin_blank_skip: (blank = skip — hosts without Webmin keep working)
provider.ping_enabled_hint: Reachability + RTT only. The sampler probes every {interval}s; latency lands in the host drawer's Ping chart.

groups.heading: Host groups
groups.intro: Collect hosts into collapsible sections in the Hosts view by matching their Custom # against a numeric range. Example: "ISP Routers" covers 1–4, "Gateways" covers 5–8. Hosts that don't fall into any range land in the "Ungrouped" bucket at the bottom.
groups.invalid_range: Invalid range for group "{name}" — end must be ≥ start.
groups.overlap: Groups "{a_name}" ({a_start}–{a_end}) and "{b_name}" ({b_start}–{b_end}) overlap. Ranges must be disjoint.
groups.empty: No groups yet. Add one below to start bucketing hosts by Custom #.
groups.nesting_hint: Nesting is 2 levels deep. A sub-group's range must fit inside its parent's range, and must not overlap any non-parent group.
groups.range_required: Range start and end are required.
groups.err_self_parent: A group cannot be its own parent.
groups.err_parent_missing: Parent "{name}" doesn't exist — type the exact name of a top-level group.
groups.err_parent_is_sub: Parent "{name}" is itself a sub-group. Nesting is limited to 2 levels.
groups.err_not_contained: Sub-group range must fit inside parent "{parent}" ({ps}–{pe}).
groups.err_overlap: Range overlaps "{other}" ({os}–{oe}). Ranges must be disjoint except for parent↔sub-group pairs.
groups.number_hint: Optional display prefix shown before the group name in the Hosts view (e.g. "32 Smart & IOT Routers"). Must be unique across all groups when set.
groups.invalid_number: Number must be a positive integer.
groups.err_number_dupe: Number already used by "{other}". Group numbers must be unique.
groups.ssh_clear_title: Erase the stored password on save
groups.ssh_hint: Resolution order: per-host override → this sub-group / parent → global default. Most-specific value wins.
groups.children_toggle_title: Collapse this parent group's sub-rows so the editor stays compact while you add more items.
groups.collapse_all_title: Hide every parent group's sub-rows so only top-level rows are visible.
groups.preview_title: Preview — how this group will render in the Hosts view.
groups.add_subgroup_title: Add a sub-group nested under "{parent}". Range pre-fills inside the parent's range so you don't trip the containment validator.

admin_assets.title: Asset inventory
admin_assets.intro: Pull an asset catalogue (vendor / model / serial / location) from your asset-management upstream. Pick one of two auth modes below. Manually refreshed — the cache at /app/data/asset_inventory.json is the source of truth the host drawer reads from.
admin_assets.auth_mode_oauth2_help: Client_credentials grant against a token endpoint; OmniGrid exchanges your web-service user + password for a short-lived bearer token on every refresh.
admin_assets.auth_mode_lifetime_help: A single static key minted once (OAuth2 with lifetime=1) and pasted in below. OmniGrid POSTs to {base_url}/services.php with X-Authorization: Bearer <key> — no token exchange at refresh time.
admin_assets.clear_secret_text: Removes the stored password. Refresh will fail until a new one is saved.
admin_assets.clear_lifetime_token_text: Removes the stored lifetime token. Refresh will fail until a new one is saved.
admin_assets.service_action_help: Two form parameters services.php routes on. For the asset catalogue, upstream expects service=assets and action=get_assets_custom_number_range — ensure the token has the assets:read scope.
admin_assets.range_help: Range bounds for actions like get_assets_custom_number_range. When both are set and action matches, OmniGrid paginates automatically in 50-batches (the server's default cap). Leave blank for actions that don't use a range.
admin_assets.edit_url_help: Prefix used by the host drawer's "Edit on upstream" link. The asset id is appended at the end (e.g. "…&si=42"). Placeholders {id} / {custom_number} / {base} are also supported when the URL needs the id mid-path. Leave blank to hide the edit link.
admin_assets.verify_tls_label: Verify TLS certificate (uncheck for self-signed asset API endpoints)
admin_assets.cache_empty: No cached snapshot. Click Refresh now to populate.

toasts_extra.sse_overflow: Live event stream backlogged — refreshed automatically
toasts_extra.prune_started: Prune started on {host} — see the floating panel.
toasts_extra.webmin_url_without_name: Row {id}: Webmin URL is set but Webmin hostname is blank. Enter the hostname the Miniserv reports (or clear the URL).
toasts_extra.custom_number_duplicate: Duplicate custom numbers — {detail}. Each host must have a unique custom number.
toasts_extra.webmin_url_without_name_inline: Webmin URL is set — hostname is required too.
toasts_extra.custom_number_duplicate_inline: Duplicate custom number (#{cn}). Each host must be unique.
toasts_extra.id_required_inline: ID is required when other fields are filled.

ssh.dry_run_ok: Dry-run OK — click Run for real to execute.
ssh.confirm_wrong_host: Hostname confirmation did not match — aborted.
ssh.destructive_hint: This command matched a destructive pattern. Type the hostname to confirm.

admin_ssh.title: SSH
admin_ssh.intro: Global SSH credentials OmniGrid uses to run one-shot commands on a host from its drawer. Admin-only, dry-run-first, audit-logged. Per-host overrides (user / port / disabled) live in Admin → Hosts under each row's SSH section.
admin_ssh.clear_key_title: Erase the stored SSH private key (and its passphrase). Cannot be undone — paste a new key after to restore access.
admin_ssh.clear_passphrase_title: Erase the stored passphrase. The private key stays; the runner will try to parse it unprotected.
admin_ssh.clear_password_title: Erase the stored default password. Per-host password overrides still apply.
admin_ssh.key_hint: An ed25519 or RSA private key in OpenSSH / PEM format. Blank on save keeps the current value. Paste a new value to replace.
admin_ssh.known_hosts_hint: Paste the target's known_hosts line(s) here to enable strict host-key verification. Leave blank to accept on first use (TOFU) — the fingerprint is always shown in the drawer so you can verify it out-of-band.
admin_ssh.custom_actions_hint: Named commands that appear as preset buttons in each host's SSH drawer. Use `{host}` in the command to substitute the target's resolved hostname at click time. Clicking a preset drops the rendered command into the textarea so you can preview before running.
admin_ssh.custom_actions_empty: No custom actions yet — add one below.
admin_ssh.destructive_hint: Regex patterns — one per line or comma-separated. Commands matching any of these require typed-hostname confirmation in the drawer. Blank falls back to the built-in list (rm / mkfs / dd / >/ / systemctl stop / reboot / poweroff / shutdown).
admin_ssh.test_widget_hint: Pick a curated host and run whoami with a short timeout. Uses the saved credentials above.

hosts_extra_ssh.action_set_nics_prompt: Enter the NIC name (e.g. eth0). The agent will be restarted after the change.
hosts_extra_ssh.disabled_not_up: SSH disabled — host is {status}. Bring the host online before running commands.
hosts_extra_ssh.confirm_prompt: Type the hostname ({host}) to confirm — this command matched a destructive pattern.

terminal.open_button_hint: Open an interactive PTY shell on this host (admin only)
terminal.cookie_expired: Session expired — please reload and sign in again
terminal.not_admin: Admin role required to open a terminal
terminal.csrf_failed: Terminal connection rejected (CSRF check failed) — refresh the page and retry
terminal.origin_mismatch: Terminal connection rejected (Origin mismatch) — your reverse-proxy isn't passing X-Forwarded-Host correctly. Check the NPM advanced configuration for this site.
terminal.not_configured: SSH is not configured for this host
terminal.xterm_missing: xterm.js failed to load — refresh the page and try again
